events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a fall protection situation where the "6-ft trigger" has been ignored. In OSHA 1926 Subpart M, the trigger height for fall protection in construction is 6 feet - meaning fall protection must be provided when working at heights of 6 feet or above. Ignoring this trigger means workers are exposed to fall hazards without protection.

This is clearly non-compliant and dangerous. The decision should be "fail" with critical risk.
